rationale: Both candidates spot the same core discrepancy the ground-truth reviewer flagged (trust_remote_code mentioned in the PR description but not reflected in the code), though neither reproduces the ground truth's specific angle (version-compat risk with kernels<0.15), so recall is capped similarly for both. X surfaces more grounded, actionable findings (missing cache test, lock serializing FA2/FA3 loads, lost 'attempting version X' log detail, dead last_error init) each tied to a specific line with a concrete fix, while Y is more concise with only two non-blocking notes and explicitly waves off the test-coverage gap that X reasonably flags. Neither fabricates findings, so precision is comparably high, but X's broader, well-grounded coverage edges out Y slightly on actionability.
